volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Rope (Section 2.3.337b - Section 2.4.415)

sections.label: Section 2.3.337b
sections.translation: In misery I pass a life not far advanced [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.3.337c
sections.translation: Do give some better omen .

sections.label: Section 2.3.338
sections.original: Verum omnes sapientes decet conferre et fabulari .
sections.translation: Still it behoves all prudent persons to confer and talk together . But , prithee , where ’ s your master , Plesidippus ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.339b
sections.translation: Marry , well said , indeed ; as if he wasn ’ t within there .

sections.label: Section 2.3.340b
sections.original: Neque pol est neque huc quidem ullus venit .
sections.translation: By my troth , he isn ’ t , nor , in fact , has he come here at all .

sections.label: Section 2.3.341
sections.translation: He hasn ’ t come ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.341b
sections.translation: You say the truth .

sections.label: Section 2.3.341c
sections.translation: That ’ s not my way , Ampelisca . But how nearly is the breakfast got ready ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.342b
sections.translation: What breakfast , I beg of you ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.343
sections.translation: The sacrifice , I mean , that you are performing here .

sections.label: Section 2.3.343b
sections.translation: Prithee , what is it you are dreaming about ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.344
sections.original: Certe huc Labrax ad prandium vocavit Plesidippum
sections.translation: For certain , Labrax invited Plesidippus hither to a breakfast , your master , my master .

sections.label: Section 2.3.345b
sections.translation: By my troth , you ’ re telling of no wondrous facts : if he has deceived Gods and men , he has only acted after the fashion of Procurers .

sections.label: Section 2.3.347
sections.original: Non rem divinam facitis hic vos neque erus ?
sections.translation: Then neither yourselves nor my master are here performing a sacrifice .

sections.label: Section 2.3.347b
sections.translation: You are a wizard .

sections.label: Section 2.3.348
sections.translation: What are you doing then ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.348b
sections.translation: The Priestess of Venus has received here into her abode both myself and Palaestra , after many mishaps and dreadful alarm , and from being in danger of our lives , destitute of aid and of resources .

sections.label: Section 2.3.351
sections.original: An hic Palaestrast , obsecro , erí mei amica ?
sections.translation: Prithee , is Palaestra here , the beloved of my master ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.351b
sections.translation: Assuredly .

sections.label: Section 2.3.352
sections.original: Inest lepos in nuntio tuo mágnus , mea Ampelisca .
sections.translation: Great joyousness is there in your news , my dear Ampelisca . But I greatly long to know what was this danger of yours .

sections.label: Section 2.3.354
sections.original: Confracta est , mi Trachalio , hac nocte navis nobis .
sections.translation: Last night our ship was wrecked , my dear Trachalio .

sections.label: Section 2.3.355
sections.translation: How , ship ? What story ’ s this ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.355b
sections.translation: Prithee , have you not heard in what way the Procurer intended secretly to carry us away hence to Sicily , and how , whatever there was at home , he placed on board ship ? That has all gone to the bottom now .

sections.label: Section 2.3.358b
sections.translation: O clever Neptune , hail to thee ! Surely , no dicer is more skilful than thyself . Decidedly

sections.label: Section 2.3.360
sections.original: nimis lépide iecisti bolum : periurum perdidisti .
sections.translation: a right pleasant throw [*] hast thou made ; thou didst break a-villain . But where now is the Procurer Labrax ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.361b
sections.translation: Perished through drinking , I suppose ; Neptune last night invited him to deep potations .

sections.label: Section 2.3.363
sections.original: Credo hercle anancaeo datum quod biberet . ut ego amo te ,
sections.translation: By my troth , I fancy it was given him to drink by way of cup of necessity [*] . How much I do love you , my dear Ampelisca ; how pleasing you are ; what honied words you do utter .

sections.label: Section 2.3.365
sections.original: sed tu et Palaestra quomodo salvae estis ?
sections.translation: But you and Palaestra , in what way were you saved ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.365b
sections.translation: I ’ ll let you know . Both in affright , we leapt from the ship into a boat , because we saw that the ship was being borne upon a rock ; in haste , I unloosed the rope , while they were in dismay . The storm separated us from them with the boat in a direction to the right . And so , tossed about by winds and waves ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.370
sections.original: iactatae exemplis plurimis miserae perpetuam noctem ;
sections.translation: in a multitude of ways , we , wretched creatures , during the livelong night ⋯ half dead , the wind this day has scarce borne us to the shore .

sections.label: Section 2.3.372
sections.original: Novi , Neptunus ita solet , quamvis fastidiosus
sections.translation: I understand ; thus is Neptune wont to do ; he is a very dainty Aedile [*] ; if any wares are bad , over he throws them all .

sections.label: Section 2.3.374_375
sections.translation: Woe to your head and life !

sections.label: Section 2.3.374_375b
sections.translation: To your own , my dear Ampelisca . I was sure that the Procurer would do that which he has done ; I often said so . It were better I should let my hair grow [*] , and set up for a soothsayer .

sections.label: Section 2.3.378
sections.original: Cavistis ergo tu atque erus ne abiret , cum scibatis .
sections.translation: Did you not take care then , you and your master , that he shouldn ’ t go away , when you knew this ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.379
sections.translation: What could he do ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.379b
sections.original: Sí amabat , rogas , quid faceret ? adservaret
sections.translation: If he was in love , do you ask what he could do ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.380
sections.original: dies nóctesque , in custodia esset semper . verum ecastor
sections.translation: Both night and day he should have kept watch ; he should have been always on his guard . But , by my troth , he has done like many others ; thus finely has Plesidippus taken care of her .

sections.label: Section 2.3.382
sections.translation: For what reason do you say that ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.382b
sections.translation: The thing is evident .

sections.label: Section 2.3.382c
sections.original: Scin tu ? etiam qui it lavatum
sections.translation: Don ’ t you know this ? Even he who goes to the bath to bathe , while there he carefully keeps an eye upon his garments , still they are stolen ; inasmuch as some one of those that he is watching is a rogue ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.385
sections.original: fur facile qui observat videt : custos qui fur sit nescit .
sections.translation: the thief easily marks him for whom he ’ s upon the watch ; the keeper knows not which one is the thief . But bring me to her ; where is she ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.386b
sections.original: I sane in Veneris fanum huc intro ,
sections.translation: Well then , go here into the Temple of Venus ; you ’ ll find her sitting there , and in tears .

sections.label: Section 2.3.387b
sections.translation: How disagreable is that to me already . But why is she weeping ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.388b
sections.original: Ego dicam tibi : hoc sése excruciat animi ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you ; she ’ s afflicting herself in mind for this ; because the Procurer took away a casket from her which she had ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.390
sections.original: qui suos parentis noscere possét : eam veretur
sections.translation: and in which she kept that by which she might be enabled to recognize her parents ; she fears that this has been lost .

sections.label: Section 2.3.391b
sections.translation: Where was that little casket , pray ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.391c
sections.translation: There , on board the ship ; he himself locked it up in his wallet , that there mightn ’ t be the means by which she might recognize her parents .

sections.label: Section 2.3.393b
sections.translation: O scandalous deed ! to require her to be a slave , who ought to be a free woman .

sections.label: Section 2.3.395
sections.original: Nunc eam cum navi scilicet abiisse pessum in altum .
sections.translation: Therefore she now laments that it has gone to the bottom along with the ship . There , too , was all the gold and silver of the Procurer .

sections.label: Section 2.3.397
sections.original: Credo aliquem immersisse atque eum excepisse .
sections.translation: Some one , I trust , has dived and brought it up .

sections.label: Section 2.3.397b
sections.translation: For this reason is she sad and disconsolate , that she has met with the loss of them .

sections.label: Section 2.3.398b
sections.translation: Then have I the greater occasion to do this , to go in and console her , that she mayn ’ t thus distress herself in mind .

sections.label: Section 2.3.400
sections.original: nam multa praeter spem scio multis bona evenisse .
sections.translation: For I know that many a lucky thing has happened to many a one beyond their hopes .

sections.label: Section 2.3.401
sections.original: At ego étiam , qui speraverint spem decepisse multos .
sections.translation: But I know too that hope has deceived many who have hoped .

sections.label: Section 2.3.402
sections.original: Ergo animus aequos optimum est aerumnae condimentum .
sections.translation: Therefore a patient mind is the best remedy for affliction . I ’ ll go in , unless you wish for anything .

sections.label: Section 2.3.403b
sections.translation: Go . ( To herself .) I ’ ll do that which the Priestess requested me , and I ’ ll ask for some water here at the neighbour ’ s ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.405
sections.original: nam extemplo , si verbis suis peterem , daturos dixit .
sections.translation: for she said that if I asked for it in her name , they would give it directly . And I do think that I never saw a more worthy old lady , one to whom I should think that it is more befitting for Gods and men to show kindness . How courteously , how heartily , how kindly , how , without the least difficulty , she received us into her home , trembling , in want , drenched , shipwrecked , half dead ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.410
sections.original: accepit ad sese , haud secus quam si ex se simus natae ;
sections.translation: not otherwise , in fact , than if we had been her own offspring . How kindly did she herself , just now , tucking up her garments , make the water warm for us to bathe . Now , that I mayn ’ t keep her waiting , I ’ ll fetch some water from the place where she requested me . ( Knocking at the door of DAEMONES .) Hallo , there , is there any one in the cottage ? is any one going to open this door ? Will any one come out ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.414
sections.original: Quís est qui nostris tám proterve fóribus facit iniúriam ?
sections.translation: Who is it so furiously making an attack upon our door ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.415
sections.translation: It ’ s I .
